rulings.Case Title: PETER PEDRETTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 12, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to dismiss as to plaintiff Paul Pedretti, Jr. for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310, 583.360 is granted. Defendant properly served Paul Pedretti, Jr. with the motion and no opposition was filed. The Court makes it clear that its ruling is only as to plaintiff Paul Pedretti, Jr. Peter Pedretti remains a plaintiff with Brayton Purcell as his counsel of record. However defendant's notice of motion explicitly only sought for its motion to be granted as to Paul Pedretti, Jr., and the Court cannot grant different relief than that stated in the notice of motion. Even if the Court could consider the motion as to Peter Pedretti, defendant's motion would be denied without prejudice for failure to demonstrate it provided Peter, through his counsel, notice of its motion. Hearing required unless defendant informs Paul Pedretti, Jr. of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Borgwarner Morse Tec Llc, As Successor-By-Merger To Borg-Warner Corporation Motion To Bifurcate Trial On The Issue Of Medical Causation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 12, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Douglass Insulation Company's joinder to BorgWarner Morse Tec LLc's motion to bifurcate trial on the issue of medical causation is denied as untimely pursuant to C.C.P. § 598. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. BorgWarner is bringing the underlying motion four months after the trial date of July 13, 2015. It failed to bring such motion more than 30 days before the trial date. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SWEIDER SR. et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 12, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B and G and denied as to Exhibits C, D, E and F. Pursuant to C.C.P. § 438, defendant failed to demonstrate that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against it because defendant failed to demonstrate plaintiffs' causes of action against it are time-barred by C.C.P. § 340.2. Plaintiffs' complaint for wrongful death and survival was timely filed within the statute of limitations period. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment substituting defendant for a Doe does not relate back to the filing of that complaint because it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of defendant's true name or the facts rendering it liable when the complaint was filed. This conclusion is the same even if the Court considers the substance of decedent's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' standard interrogatory responses. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
